name: Pecan-Coated Roast Pork Loin with Baked Peaches
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub the pork thoroughly with olive oil.
2. Combine the sage, thyme, garlic, salt, pepper, and 1 tablespoon of the brown sugar in a food processor or blender. Pulse until you have a thick paste. You may need to add a drizzle of olive oil to get it started.
3. Spread the paste over the pork loin and cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate overnight.
4. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
5. Roll the pork loin in the chopped peanuts and place it in a roasting pan. Make a tent of aluminum foil and arrange it over the pork loin, covering the nuts completely so they won't char. Roast for 30 minutes.
6. Lower the heat to 350°F (175°C). After 30 more minutes, remove the foil and place the peach halves around the pork loin in the roasting pan. Sprinkle with remaining brown sugar and a grinding of nutmeg.
7. Continue to roast until the pork is done, about 20 more minutes.
